channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Drishyam 3 review: Mohanlal reprises Georgekutty but the story drags
item.description: Drishyam 3, the latest installment in Jeethu Joseph’s Malayalam thriller series, sees Mohanlal returning as Georgekutty, the clever rubber farmer who previously outwitted the law to protect his family...

item.title: NAHCO declares N21.18bn dividends and bonus shares after strong 2025 results
item.description: Shareholders of Nigerian Aviation Handling Company Plc (NAHCO) don approve total payout of around N21.18 billion for 2025, wey include both cash dividends and bonus shares. For their 45th Annual Gener...

item.title: FBI Director Kash Patel may qualify for compensation from Trump-linked fund
item.description: The New York Times reports that FBI Director Kash Patel could potentially receive millions from a controversial $1.8 billion fund reportedly linked to former President Donald Trump. The fund, original...

item.title: Malacañang assures lawful enforcement of arrest warrant for Senator Bato dela Rosa
item.description: Malacañang confirmed on Thursday that the government will strictly follow the law in implementing the arrest warrant issued against Senator Ronald 'Bato' Dela Rosa. Palace Press Officer Undersecretary...

item.title: Bureau of Meteorology forecasts warmer and drier winter for much of Australia amid possible El Niño
item.description: The Bureau of Meteorology (BoM) has released its first winter outlook for 2026, signalling that much of Australia could face a warmer and drier season due to the likely development of El Niño. Despite...

item.title: Celebrating Maria McCloy: A Legacy of Creativity and Connection in South Africa
item.description: Maria McCloy, a celebrated multidisciplinary creative, was honoured at a memorial service at the Joburg Theatre, where industry colleagues, friends, and family gathered to reflect on her remarkable co...

item.title: Canada and Europe Seek Greater Influence in Global Film Industry at Cannes
item.description: At the Cannes Film Festival, industry leaders are signalling a shift in global cinematic influence away from the United States. Daniela Elstner of Unifrance emphasises that the traditional dominance o...

item.title: WHO Raises Alarm Over Rapid Spread of Rare Ebola Strain in Eastern Congo
item.description: The World Health Organization (WHO) has raised serious concerns over a rapidly spreading outbreak of a rare Ebola strain, Bundibugyo, in eastern Congo. Authorities have reported at least 134 suspected...

item.title: Unemployment hits four-year high as political tensions rise over bracket creep
item.description: In the latest episode of Newsworthy, reporter Stephen Johnson highlights that unemployment in Australia has reached its highest point in four years, with 33,000 Australians currently unable to find wo...

item.title: CDF Munir Reaffirms Pakistan’s Commitment to Combating Terrorism Nationwide
item.description: Chief of Defence Forces (CDF) and Army Chief, Field Marshal Asim Munir, on Thursday reiterated Pakistan's firm commitment to continue the fight against terrorism with full national resolve. Speaking a...

item.title: New Zealand Warriors Secure Key Halves with Re-signings
item.description: The New Zealand Warriors are solidifying their halves lineup for the upcoming seasons with the confirmation of one re-signing and progress on another. Following Luke Metcalf's planned departure to the...

item.title: Israeli authorities detain Gaza aid activists during flotilla interception
item.description: On Wednesday, Israeli police intercepted a flotilla carrying aid to Gaza and compelled the activists aboard to kneel on the ground with their hands tied behind their backs. This action took place unde...

item.title: Retirement flat owners face soaring service charges and falling resale values
item.description: Many owners of retirement flats across England and Wales are finding themselves financially trapped due to escalating service charges and declining property values. Michael Fraser, who moved into a re...

item.title: Robert De Niro Delivers Sharp Critique of Trump During Stephen Colbert’s Last 'Colbert Questionert' Segment
item.description: During the second-to-last episode of “The Late Show” on May 21, 2026, host Stephen Colbert’s usual celebrity segment, the “Colbert Questionert,” took an unexpected turn when Oscar-winning actor Robert...

item.title: FAO Offers Paid Internship Opportunities for Young Africans in 2026
item.description: The Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) of the United Nations is calling on young Kenyans and other African nationals aged 21 to 30 to apply for its 2026 Internship Programme. This is a paid oppor...

item.title: Ontario colleges face declining international enrolment following federal visa policy changes
item.description: Ontario's colleges are experiencing a significant decline in international student enrolment following federal government restrictions on study permits. Introduced less than two years ago, the cap on ...

item.title: ODM Youth Leader Faces Backlash for Criticising Eric Omondi During Finance Bill Discussion
item.description: During a Finance Bill 2026/2027 discussion led by National Treasury CS John Mbadi on May 20, ODM Young Captains chairperson Roseline Alionya was strongly heckled after criticising comedian Eric Omondi...

item.title: How Palarong Pambansa Shapes Future Filipino Sports Champions
item.description: As the 2026 Palarong Pambansa approaches, the Department of Education (DepEd) highlights the competition’s role in developing Filipino athletes who later succeed internationally. Many top athletes beg...

item.title: Chinwe Egwim Joins FirstBank as Chief Economist to Strengthen Economic Strategy
item.description: FirstBank don appoint Chinwe Egwim as their new Chief Economist, and dis move dey show say economic intelligence don become very key for Nigeria banking sector. Banks dey face wahala like high inflati...

item.title: Sowore Raises Alarm Over Alleged Illegal Allocation of Jabi Lake Park Land by FCT Minister Wike
item.description: Human rights activist Omoyele Sowore don raise alarm say FCT Minister Nyesom Wike don allocate parts of Jabi Lake Park land to himself, including about three hectares, while telling other people wey d...
